data.title: Lazard to buy private equity advisory firm Campbell Lutyens in $575 million deal creating  new private capital unit
data.description:
Investment bank Lazard said on Thursday that it has entered into an agreement to acquire private equity advisory firm Campbell Lutyens for a total consideration of about $575 million, creating a new private capital advisory division called Lazard CL.

With Lazard's existing private capital advisory group and Campbell Lutyens, the new unit will include fundraising, secondary advisory, and capital advisory businesses in infrastructure, private credit, private equity, and real estate, the company said.

Lazard CL will be formed as the investment bank's third global business, and will be co-led by Holcombe Green, the global head of private capital advisory at Lazard and Gordon Bajnai, the current CEO of Campbell Lutyens.

The new unit will offer clients advisory services across the full capital life cycle, from formation to liquidity to strategic alternatives, the company said, as it pairs combined proprietary datasets of Lazard's private capital group and Campbell Lutyens.

"This transaction marks another defining strategic step on the path toward Lazard 2030 and an exciting avenue for future growth," CEO Peter Orszag said.

Lazard said that along with the consideration of about $575 million, there is also a potential of additional consideration of up to $85 million to be paid based on defined performance criteria over a multi-year period.

The deal is expected to be accretive to Lazard's 2027 earnings and thereafter, the investment bank said.

data.title: ArcelorMittal Eyes Boost From EU's Doubled Steel Tariffs
data.description:
(05:00 GMT) *ArcelorMittal 1Q Sales $15.46B
(05:01 GMT) *ArcelorMittal 1Q EBITDA $1.68B
(05:01 GMT) *Analysts Saw ArcelorMittal 1Q Ebitda at $1.655 Bln
(05:01 GMT) *ArcelorMittal 1Q Net Pft $575M
(05:01 GMT) *Analysts Saw ArcelorMittal 1Q Net Pft at $498M
(05:02 GMT) *ArcelorMittal 1Q EPS $0.76
(05:02 GMT) *Analysts Saw ArcelorMittal 1Q EPS at 65 Cents
(05:03 GMT) *ArcelorMittal 1Q Crude Steel Production 13.3M Metric Tons
(05:03 GMT) *ArcelorMittal 1Q Steel Shipments 12.8M Tons
(05:04 GMT) *ArcelorMittal Sees Support for FCF, Shareholder Returns This Year
(05:04 GMT) *ArcelorMittal: Fundamentals Have Improved Over Recent Months
(05:04 GMT) *ArcelorMittal Sees Boost From European Policy, Including New Tariff Regime
(05:20 GMT) ArcelorMittal Eyes Boost From EU's Doubled Steel Tariffs

By Joshua Kirby

ArcelorMittal said higher European tariffs on steel imports can boost its production and earnings ahead, as net profit continued to slide at the start of the year.

The Luxembourg-based steelmaker said net profit came to $575 million for the January-March period, down from $805 million in the same period last year. The result was nonetheless better than the $498 million forecast by analysts, according to a consensus of estimates provided by the company. Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortization--a key profit metric for the group--rose to $1.68 billion, up from $1.58 billion in the year-prior period.

Crude steel production came to 13.3 million metric tons over the period, down from 14.8 million tons.

The company said it is confident that production will get a boost from higher European Union import tariffs due to come into effect as soon as July 1. The EU this month approved a plan to double tariffs on steel imports from outside the bloc in a bid to protect domestic production.

"ArcelorMittal is well positioned to capture this upside through existing capacity and by re-starting idled capacity," Chief Executive Aditya Mittal said.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.description:

(05:00 GMT) *DHL Group: Co-Compiled Consensus Saw 1Q Revenue at EUR20.71B
(05:00 GMT) *DHL Group 1Q EBIT EUR1.48B

(05:00 GMT) *DHL Group 1Q Net Pft EUR812M

(05:00 GMT) *DHL Group Expects Geopolitical Uncertainties to Persist Throughout 2026
(05:00 GMT) *DHL Group Backs 2025 EBIT Guidance of At Least EUR6.2B
(05:00 GMT) *DHL Group Backs 2025 Cash Flow Guidance of Around EUR3B
(05:00 GMT) *DHL Group: Will Continue to Focus on Efficiency Improvements and Investments For Future Growth
(05:09 GMT) *Correct: DHL Group 1Q Rev EUR20.42B
(05:09 GMT) *Correct: DHL Group 1Q EBIT EUR1.48B
(05:09 GMT) *Correct: DHL Group 1Q Net Pft EUR812M
(05:21 GMT) DHL Group Backs Guidance Despite Expecting Continued Uncertainty

By Dominic Chopping

DHL parent Deutsche Post still expects earnings to rise this year, despite anticipating global geopolitical uncertainty throughout it.

The German logistics company--also known as DHL Group--backed guidance for earnings before interest and taxes of at least 6.2 billion euros ($7.24 billion) this year and free cash flow of around 3 billion euros.

"We are well on track to achieve our full-year targets," said Chief Executive Tobias Meyer. "Our successful start to the year highlights the resilience of our business model and the impact of our efficiency measures."

The company said it continues to invest in operational efficiency and in regions and sectors with strong customer demand. It is expanding its capabilities in the data center market and aims to add more than 10 additional warehouse sites in North America by the end of 2026.

The new facilities will serve data-center operators that require reliable and secure supply chains as they expand rapidly, it said.

The group made a net profit of 812 million euros in the first quarter compared with 786 million euros in the same period a year earlier.

Revenue fell 1.9% to 20.42 billion euros.

A company-compiled consensus had forecast revenue of 20.71 billion euros and net profit of 764 million euros.

Write to Dominic Chopping at dominic.chopping@wsj.com

data.title: BASF Profit Rises, Sales Drop on Currency Headwinds
data.description:
By Nina Kienle

Germany's BASF posted higher first-quarter profit but lower sales due to strong adverse currency effects and slightly lower prices.

The chemical giant on Thursday reported net profit for the first three months of the year of 927 million euros ($1.08 billion) from 808 million euros in the prior-year period. The figure missed analysts' forecast of 1.03 billion euros, according to consensus estimates provided by the company.

Sales for the period declined by 3% to 16.02 billion euros, compared with analysts' estimated 15.88 billion euros.

"Currency effects, mainly the U.S. dollar and Chinese renminbi, had a negative influence on sales in all segments," BASF said.

Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, amortization and special items fell 5.6% to 2.36 billion euros.

Meanwhile, earnings before interest and taxes rose to 1.26 billion euros from 1.16 billion euros.

The Ludwigshafen-based company backed its outlook for the year.

Write to Nina Kienle at nina.kienle@wsj.com

data.title: META Stock Rout Deepens Overnight On Capex Woes — Analysts, Retail Bulls Still Back Mag 7’s Cheapest AI Bet
data.description:
Key points:
  • CFO Susan Li said the higher capex guidance comes amid rising memory costs and expanded data center investment.
  • Despite the selloff, analyst Gary Black said that most unit-level operating metrics exceeded expectations.
  • Investor caution also reflects ongoing losses at Meta’s Reality Labs unit, which reported another $4 billion in quarterly operating loss.

Shares of Meta Platforms, Inc. (META) slid more than 7% in overnight trading on Thursday after the company raised its capex outlook for this year, even as several analysts and retail investors argued that the sell-off overlooked strong engagement and early momentum from its latest AI rollout.

META stock is poised to record its best month in a year. On Wednesday, shares fell 0.3% to end at 66.12 and fell 7% in extended trading after releasing their quarterly results.

META Selloff Looks Overdone 

Meta projected 2026 capex in the range of $125 billion to $145 billion, up from its earlier forecast of $115 billion to $135 billion, as part of its push to scale infrastructure for rising AI needs. The higher spending outlook overshadowed solid results across advertising, engagement and AI product development.

CFO Susan Li said on the company’s earnings call that the updated outlook was driven primarily by higher memory prices and additional data center costs required to support future model training and inference capacity.

However, Future Fund Managing Partner Gary Black said on X that Meta “shouldn’t be down” following the updated capex guidance, as most unit-level operating metrics across the business came in ahead of expectations.

Analyst Sees Hidden Strength In META Usage 

Concerns around user activity were also tempered after Meta attributed the decline in daily active users to internet disruptions in Iran and restrictions on WhatsApp access in Russia. However, CEO Mark Zuckerberg said that trends on Instagram and Facebook remained strong despite the disruptions, with video continuing to drive record engagement on both platforms.

Deepwater Management founder Gene Munster said on X that the estimated growth would likely have been closer to 5.5% after adjusting for the geopolitical disruptions.

Munster also pointed to early engagement improvements from Meta’s latest AI model rollout, saying that Meta’s Muse Spark model is already improving user sessions by a “double-digit” percentage. Launched earlier this month, Muse Spark is Meta’s first AI model from its new Meta Superintelligence Labs and powers its Meta AI assistant, recommendation and engagement features across its apps.

“AI is going to make it even more difficult for humanity to put down their screens,” Munster added.

Reality Labs Burn Raises Investor Concerns 

Investor caution around Meta’s rising capex has also been spurred by continued losses and restructuring within its Reality Labs unit, which houses the company’s virtual reality and hardware initiatives.

The segment reported another $4 billion operating loss in the first quarter, extending cumulative losses to roughly $83 billion since 2020. Charlie Bilello, director of research at Pension Partners, weighed in on losses at Reality Labs and said on X, “We've never seen a public company light money on fire to this extent.”

The losses come alongside broader restructuring inside the division. Meta slashed over 1,000 Reality Labs roles earlier this year and followed that with additional layoffs affecting several hundred employees last month across hardware, recruiting and sales teams as resources were redirected toward AI infrastructure and wearable technologies.

Munster said that Zuckerberg is “trying to smooth over the fact that Meta has been the most aggressive company at replacing humans with AI.” He estimated that the company has cut 15% of its headcount over the past year.

How Do Retail Traders Feel About META?

On Stocktwits, retail sentiment for META jumped to ‘extremely bullish’ from ‘neutral’ levels after the results, amid nearly a 600% surge in 24-hour message volume.

One user noted that Meta has “much lower PE than other Mag 7, and the only company shown it has profitable AI spend.”

https://stocktwits.com/Chalkstocks/message/651839224

Meta’s valuation also remains the most attractive within the “Magnificent Seven” peer group, with the stock trading at a forward P/E of about 22.1x, below Microsoft's 24.2x and Amazon's about 33.9x.

Another user echoed a similar sentiment, saying, “580-590. Buy zone. Best valued faang.”

https://stocktwits.com/iMikeCorleone/message/651838426

META stock has risen 1.4% so far this year and is up 21% over the past 12 months.

data.title: Benchmark JGB yield hits 29-year high as oil surge fuels inflation fears
data.description:
By Satoshi Sugiyama

Japanese government bond yields rose on Thursday, with the benchmark 10-year yield hitting a 29-year high, as reports of U.S. military action to end Iran stalemate drove oil to a four-year high and fuelled inflation concerns.

The benchmark 10-year JGB yield (JP10YTN=JBTC) rose 6.5 basis points to 2.525%, the highest since June 1997. The five-year yield (JP5YTN=JBTC) rose 5 bps to a record high of 1.905%.

Yields move inversely to bond prices.

Brent crude futures < LCOc1 > jumped $8 to $126.09 a barrel by 0417 GMT, after a 6.1% rise on Wednesday.

"Higher oil prices and the risk of further deterioration in the situation around the Strait of Hormuz are weighing on the market, leading to stronger selling pressure in the long end of the bond market," said Ryutaro Kimura, fixed-income strategist at BNP Paribas Asset Management.

"It raises the risk of further price increases, and, judging from global market scenarios as well, the current move is likely to heighten inflation concerns."

The Bank of Japan's reluctance to signal a near-term rate hike, after keeping interest rates steady on Tuesday, also reinforced pressure on yields at the long end, Kimura said.

Meanwhile, the two-year JGB yield (JP2YTN=JBTC), which is most sensitive to BOJ policy, pared early gains to 1.375%, up 0.5 bp on the day.

Demand was strong at an auction of about 2.8 trillion yen ($17.45 billion) of the notes, with a bid-to-cover ratio of 5.24, the highest since August 2024.

Kimura said the auction results signalled less about yield appeal than investor caution, with funds rotating into shorter maturities as a defensive play amid growing concern over rising-rate risk in long- and super-long bonds.

The 30-year yield (JP30YTN=JBTC) added 6 bps to 3.7%. The yield on the 40-year JGB (JP40YTN=JBTC), Japan's longest tenor, rose 7 bps to 3.935%.

Separately, Reuters reported that Japan's government is considering reinstating electricity and natural gas subsidies for three months starting in July, with funding of up to about 500 billion yen, to be drawn from reserve funds rather than a supplementary budget.

($1 = 160.4700 yen)

data.title: Norway's Elkem Q1 EBITDA drops 65%, misses estimates amid weak demand and low sales
data.description:
Overview

  • Norway silicon-based materials maker's Q1 EBITDA fell sharply, missing analyst expectations

  • Q1 operating income declined 7% yr/yr amid weak demand and low sales prices

  • Company to sell Silicones division, launch NOK 1.5 bln equity offering, and refinance EUR 1 bln debt

Outlook

  • Silicon Products results expected to improve gradually as production returns to full capacity

  • Second quarter to be affected by costs associated with restarting production

  • Carbon Solutions anticipates generally stable financial performance in the second quarter

Result Drivers

  • PRODUCTION CURTAILMENTS - Co said lower Q1 results were mainly due to production curtailments at several Norwegian plants, including complete stops at Elkem Rana and Elkem Salten

  • WEAK DEMAND AND LOW PRICES - Market conditions were characterized by weak demand and low sales prices, impacting operating income and division results

  • SALES MIX AND CURRENCY - Carbon Solutions' lower EBITDA was mainly due to negative impact from sales mix and currency, as well as the production curtailments in Silicon Products

Company press release:

Analyst Coverage

  • The current average analyst rating on the shares is "buy" and the breakdown of recommendations is 5 "strong buy" or "buy", 2 "hold" and no "sell" or "strong sell"

  • The average consensus recommendation for the specialty chemicals peer group is "buy"

  • Wall Street's median 12-month price target for Elkem ASA is NOK33.00, about 19.6% above its April 29 closing price of NOK27.60

  • The stock recently traded at 22 times the next 12-month earnings vs. a P/E of 32 three months ago

data.title: Cimsa Cimento Q1 Net Profit Jumps To 640.7 Mln Lira YoY
data.description:
ÇİMSA ÇİMENTO SANAYİ VE TİCARET :

  • SAID ON WEDNESDAY Q1 NET PROFIT OF 640.7 MILLION LIRA VERSUS NET PROFIT OF 355.1 MILLION LIRA YEAR AGO

  • Q1 REVENUE OF 11.6 BILLION LIRA VERSUS 11.7 BILLION LIRA YEAR AGO
